criteria.criterion: scripts/delegation_report.py prints a single JSON object with keys codex_attempts_by_risk_class (low/default/high resolved from <specs-dir>/<spec_id>.yaml, missing spec/risk_class → 'unclassified'), codex_attempts_total, codex_merged_total (result.json merged true), claude_direct_merges (merge commits whose branch does NOT start with codex/), codex_branch_merges (branch starts with codex/), and window
criteria.evidence: scripts/delegation_report.py:169-183 builds the report dict with exactly those six keys and prints one json.dumps object. read_risk_class (lines ~29-52) reads <specs_dir>/<spec_id>.yaml and returns 'unclassified' on missing file, missing/unrecognized risk_class, or parse failure; scan_attempts counts merged only when result.json has merged is True (line ~103); scan_merges/merged_branch classify merges by codex/ prefix. Test asserts codex_attempts_by_risk_class == {low:1, default:1, high:1, unclassified:1}, totals 4/1, codex_branch_merges 1, claude_direct_merges 1 (tests/delegation_report.sh:73-96).

criteria.criterion: The JSON output contains NO field named delegation_ratio and no other single-scalar ratio of delegated to direct work
criteria.evidence: Report dict at scripts/delegation_report.py:169-183 contains only counts and the window description object — no ratio field of any kind. Test asserts set(report) == expected six keys and 'delegation_ratio' not in raw output (tests/delegation_report.sh:78-95).

criteria.criterion: With missing/empty .orchestrator/attempts and/or no merge commits, the script exits 0 and prints valid JSON with zero counts
criteria.evidence: attempt_directories returns [] when attempts_dir is not a directory (scripts/delegation_report.py:56-58); scan_merges returns 0,0 when git log fails or emits no merges (lines ~139-155); main always returns 0. Test's empty case uses a nonexistent attempts dir, empty specs dir, and a one-commit repo with no merges, and asserts all five counts are 0 (tests/delegation_report.sh:98-115); test_command exited 0.

criteria.criterion: Accepts optional --attempts-dir, --specs-dir, --repo overrides, is read-only (creates/modifies no files), and does not import or execute scripts/dispatch.py
criteria.evidence: parse_args defines all three flags with repo-root defaults (scripts/delegation_report.py:157-163). Only imports are stdlib (argparse, json, re, subprocess, pathlib) plus optional yaml; no reference to dispatch.py anywhere; the only subprocess calls are read-only `git branch --show-current` and `git log`. The test snapshots the full input tree (type/mode/size/mtime) before and after the run and fails if anything changed (tests/delegation_report.sh:53-60).

criteria.criterion: tests/delegation_report.sh builds a synthetic attempts dir (low-risk merged, default-risk unmerged, attempt missing result.json) with matching spec files, plus a throwaway repo with one codex/* merge and one non-codex merge, asserts counts and the empty-input case, and exits 0
criteria.evidence: put_attempt creates SPEC-LOW merged=true, SPEC-DEFAULT merged=false, SPEC-HIGH with no result.json, plus SPEC-UNKNOWN, each with a matching specs/*.yaml (tests/delegation_report.sh:23-30); repo gets a 'Merge branch codex/SPEC-LOW-1' merge and a 'Merge branch claude/control-plane' merge (lines 39-49); count assertions at lines 73-96, empty-input assertions at lines 98-115; script runs under set -euo pipefail and ends with echo PASS. Orchestrator evidence: bash tests/delegation_report.sh exited 0.

scope_finding: Only scripts/delegation_report.py and tests/delegation_report.sh are touched — both listed in in_scope; no out-of-scope paths (dispatch.py, scripts/lib, .orchestrator, .github, specs) appear in the diff. Orchestrator scope check: PASS.
regression_finding: None observed — the diff adds two new files only and modifies no existing code, so no existing behaviour can regress. The report script is purely read-only (git log/branch and file reads).
security_findings: None observed. No secrets, no network use (needs_network false is honored), subprocess is limited to read-only git invocations with list-form argv (no shell=True), and the test operates entirely in a mktemp sandbox cleaned via trap. The yaml import fallback degrades to a narrow regex rather than executing anything.
reasons: All five acceptance criteria are met with direct evidence in the diff, and the orchestrator confirms bash tests/delegation_report.sh exited 0.

The output schema matches the spec exactly (six keys, unclassified bucket present, no delegation_ratio or any other scalar ratio), honoring the CLAUDE.md invariant against a single delegation-ratio target.
Read-only behaviour is not just claimed but enforced by the test's before/after filesystem snapshot, and the script neither imports nor executes dispatch.py.
Empty-input handling (missing attempts dir, no merges) is implemented defensively and covered by an explicit test case.
Changes stay strictly within the approved scope with no regression or security concerns.
